#### What success looks like

**(Best)** One or more sorries fully closed. Sorry trajectory `6 → 5` or better.

**(Good)** Substantial scaffolding landed (e.g., a per-basic-open localisation-compatibility helper drafted, `cotangent_exact_sequence` decomposed into named `surjective` and `exact` sub-claims). Trajectory unchanged or +1 from finer decomposition.

**(Acceptable)** A sorry decomposed into finer substeps with clear commentary. Iter-067 pickup is unambiguous.

**(Failure)** No engagement beyond iter-065: all 6 sorries unchanged, no new scaffolding.

1	\chapter{The sheaf of relative differentials}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the sheaf of relative K\"ahler differentials
5	$\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $X \to S$, starting from the
6	ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already
7	available in Mathlib. The output is a quasi-coherent sheaf of
8	$\struct{X}$-modules characterised by its restriction to affine opens:
9	for any affine open $V = \Spec B \subseteq X$ lying over an affine open
10	$U = \Spec A \subseteq S$, the sections satisfy
11	\[
12	  \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{B/A}.
13	\]
14	
15	The downstream role of this chapter is twofold. First, the smoothness
16	hypothesis on the curve $C \to \Spec k$ that appears in the Jacobian
17	definition and the Abel--Jacobi map amounts to the condition that
18	$\Omega_{C/k}$ is locally free of rank one; this characterisation is
19	what unlocks the dimension computation
20	$\genus(C) = \dim_k H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C})$.
21	Second, the cotangent space at the identity section of $\Jac(C)$ is
22	$\Omega_{\Jac(C)/k}$ pulled back along the unit $e \colon \Spec k \to
23	\Jac(C)$; the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ that appears in the
24	smoothness statement is read off from this tangent space.
25	
26	\section{The relative cotangent sheaf}
27	
28	\begin{definition}\leanok
29	[Relative cotangent presheaf of an algebra]
30	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
31	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
32	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. For each pair of
33	  affine opens $U \subseteq S$, $V \subseteq X$ with $f(V) \subseteq U$,
34	  write $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$, and use the
35	  $f$-induced ring map $A \to B$. The K\"ahler differential module
36	  $\Omega_{B/A}$ is then a $B$-module, hence a section over $V$ of a
37	  presheaf on $X$. The collection of these modules, together with the
38	  natural restriction maps along inclusions of affine opens, defines a
39	  presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$.
40	\end{definition}
41	
42	\begin{definition}\leanok
43	[Relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$]
44	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
45	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentials}
46	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
47	  The presheaf above is in fact a sheaf in the Zariski topology on
48	  $X$: the K\"ahler differential module commutes with localisation,
49	  which gives the gluing axiom on affine opens, and the descent to
50	  non-affine opens is standard. The resulting sheaf of
51	  $\struct{X}$-modules is the \emph{sheaf of relative differentials}
52	  $\Omega_{X/S}$. It is quasi-coherent.
53	\end{definition}
54	
55	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
56	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
57	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module
58	  $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d}
59	  \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on
60	  affine opens is, by definition, this Mathlib module. The
61	  derivative-of-localisation isomorphism $\Omega_{B[1/f]/A} \cong
62	  \Omega_{B/A} \otimes_B B[1/f]$ is precisely what makes the
63	  affine-restriction presheaf into a sheaf.
64	\end{remark}
65	
66	\section{Universal property and the cotangent exact sequence}
67	
68	\begin{definition}\leanok
69	[Universal derivation]
70	  \label{def:universal_derivation}
71	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.universalDerivation}
72	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
73	  The morphism of sheaves of abelian groups
74	  $\mathrm{d} \colon \struct{X} \to \Omega_{X/S}$, defined on each
75	  affine open $V = \Spec B$ over $\Spec A$ by the Mathlib universal
76	  derivation $\mathrm{d}_{B/A} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$, is the
77	  \emph{universal derivation}. It is an $A$-linear sheaf map satisfying
78	  the Leibniz rule $\mathrm{d}(ab) = a \mathrm{d}(b) + b \mathrm{d}(a)$.
79	\end{definition}
80	
81	% NOTE (iter-065 review): the Lean statement contains a `(by sorry)` inside
82	% the `CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β (by sorry)` argument; the existential
83	% bundle therefore does not yet assert $\alpha \circ \beta = 0$ mathematically
84	% until the nested sorry is closed (or the statement is re-shaped to expose
85	% the composition-zero proof obligation as a separate slot).
86	\begin{theorem}\leanok
87	[Cotangent exact sequence for a composition]
88	  \label{thm:cotangent_exact_sequence}
89	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_exact_sequence}
90	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf, def:universal_derivation}
91	  For schemes $X \xrightarrow{f} Y \xrightarrow{g} S$, there is an
92	  exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
93	  \[
94	    f^*\Omega_{Y/S} \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\longrightarrow\;
95	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0,
96	  \]
97	  built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
98	  sequence and glued via the compatibility above.
99	\end{theorem}
100	
101	\section{Smoothness and local freeness of $\Omega$}
102	
103	\begin{theorem}\leanok
104	[Smoothness in terms of $\Omega$]
105	  \label{thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
106	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
107	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
108	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a finite-presentation morphism of schemes.
109	  Then $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ if and only if
110	  $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $n$ as a sheaf of
111	  $\struct{X}$-modules. The forward direction is the Jacobian
112	  criterion; the converse follows from the cotangent exact sequence
113	  and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point.
114	\end{theorem}
115	
116	\begin{corollary}\leanok
117	[Cotangent space at a section]
118	  \label{cor:cotangent_at_section}
119	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_at_section}
120	  \uses{thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
121	  If $f \colon X \to S$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and
122	  $s \colon S \to X$ is a section of $f$, then the cotangent space
123	  $s^*\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$.
124	  In particular, if $S = \Spec k$ is a field point and $X$ is smooth of
125	  relative dimension $n$, the cotangent space at the corresponding
126	  $k$-point is an $n$-dimensional $k$-vector space.
127	\end{corollary}
128	
129	\section{Curves and the canonical sheaf}
130	
131	The case of a smooth proper curve $C \to \Spec k$ is the special case
132	$n = 1$: the relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{C/k}$ is a line bundle
133	on $C$, classically denoted $\omega_C$ and called the \emph{canonical
134	sheaf}.
135	
136	\begin{theorem}\leanok
137	[Serre-duality genus equality]
138	  \label{thm:serre_duality_genus}
139	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.serre_duality_genus}
140	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
141	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a
142	  field $k$. Then
143	  \[
144	    \dim_k H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C}).
145	  \]
146	  Both sides equal the genus $\genus(C)$. The equality is the dimension
147	  one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical sheaf; combined
148	  with the definition $\genus(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C})$, this
149	  provides the alternative differential-theoretic characterisation used
150	  when computing the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ at the identity.
151	\end{theorem}
152

After iter-059 lands, the iter-060+ substantive Koszul branch has all four pieces of the localization argument packaged as named declarations: (i) the cover constructor $\mathtt{basicOpenCover}$ (iter-054); (ii) the affine-membership $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_finset\_inf'\_isAffineOpen}$ (iter-057); (iii) the inclusion $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_finset\_inf'\_le}$ (iter-058); (iv) the localization $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_finset\_inf'\_isLocalization}$ (iter-059). The iter-060+ obligation is the actually-substantive content: chain these into a complex-level acyclicity proof via the \emph{extra-degeneracy} engine, see the iter-060+ scoping section below.

The Čech cochain complex of the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_C$ with respect to this cover is

1445-  \uses{def:Scheme_basicOpenCover, def:Scheme_IsCechAcyclicCover, thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_finset_inf_eq_basicOpen_prod, thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_finset_inf_le, thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_finset_inf_isLocalization}
1446-  Let $k$ be a field, $C$ a $k$-scheme, $U \subseteq C$ an affine open, and $s \subseteq \mathcal O_C(U)$ a subset such that the ideal generated by $s$ in $\mathcal O_C(U)$ is the unit ideal. Then the Čech cochain complex of the structure sheaf with respect to the basic-open cover $\mathfrak U = \{D(f)\}_{f \in s}$ of $U$ is acyclic in positive degrees: for every $n \ge 1$,

1474:\textbf{Mathlib leverage.} Step~1 uses the local-to-global theorem for spanning sets (\texttt{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span} or \texttt{exact\_of\_localized\_span} in \texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean}). Step~2 uses the structure-sheaf-section-as-localization theorem for affine basic opens (\texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_of\_eq\_basicOpen} in \texttt{Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean}), already packaged for the project's $n$-ary intersection by Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_finset_inf_isLocalization}, plus exactness preservation under localization (\texttt{IsLocalizedModule.map\_exact} in \texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean}). Step~3 uses the extra-degeneracy contraction theorem (\texttt{ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv} in \texttt{Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean}) packaged for Čech objects (\texttt{FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech} in \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean}). Mathlib's extra-degeneracy is set up on augmented simplicial objects valued in chain complexes; the project's Čech complex, built on the cosimplicial side via the alternating-coface map, requires a routine $\op$-passage to apply the chain-complex statement to the cochain complex.
1475-
1476:\textbf{Note on Mathlib coverage.} Mathlib does not currently host a formalisation of the Koszul complex or of any Čech-to-derived-functor spectral sequence (Stacks tags 03OU, 01EW). The proof above bypasses both by working only with the augmented complex on each basic-open patch and the extra-degeneracy contraction --- the same route used in the standard pre-Koszul textbook proofs. The Čech-to-derived-functor comparison statement (i.e.\ the statement that, under the acyclicity established here, Čech cohomology agrees with the derived-functor cohomology) is a separate mathematical theorem whose formalisation is queued for a later iteration as a project-local construction, again as a direct "augmented Čech complex is a resolution of $\mathcal O_C(U)$" argument rather than a full spectral sequence.
1477-
1478-\subsection{Use in Serre finiteness}
1479-
1480:The iter-029 lemmas materialise Step 1 (the abstract LES) and Step 2 (the corner-identification simp lemmas) of the iter-028 Serre-finiteness sketch (Section~\ref{sec:scheme_affineCoverMVSquare}); iter-030 materialised Step 2.5 (the toModuleKSheaf specialisation, a routine binding so downstream code paths can reference the LES on the structure sheaf of a curve directly); iter-031 materialised the \emph{source-object} half of Step 3 (the cover-totality identification at the sheaf level); iter-032 materialised the \emph{Ext-transport} half of Step 3 at universe $u+1$ (landing on $\HModule\,k\,F\,n$); iter-033 materialised the parallel \emph{Ext-transport} at universe $u$ (landing on $\Ext_u\,X_2\,F\,n$); iter-034 closed Step 3 by appending the Mathlib gap-fill $\Ext.\mathtt{chgUnivLinearEquiv}$ (universe-bump $\LinearEquiv$ upgrade of Mathlib's bare $\Equiv$ $\Ext.\mathtt{chgUniv}$) and composing iter-033 with it to produce the full bridge $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,T \equiv_{\Module k} \HModule\,k\,F\,n$ for terminal $T$; iter-035 closed Step 3.5 (the \emph{corner-bridge}) by specialising the iter-034 universal bridge to the $X_4$ corner of an $\AffineCoverMVSquare$ — both an abstract sheaf form (Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_HModule_prime_X4_linearEquiv}) and a curve specialisation (Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_HModule_prime_X4_linearEquiv_curve}); iter-036 appended the immediate \emph{finrank corollary} (Theorems~\ref{thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_finrank_HModule_eq_HModule_prime_X4} and~\ref{thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_finrank_HModule_eq_HModule_prime_X4_curve}) — landing the genus carrier $\mathrm{finrank}_k\,(\HModule\,k\,(\toModuleKSheaf\,C)\,1)$ on the explicit corner $X_4$ via $\LinearEquiv.\mathtt{finrank\_eq}$; iter-037 appends the parallel \emph{$\Module.\mathtt{Finite}$ transport companion} (Theorems~\ref{thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_module_finite_HModule_of_HModule_prime_X4} and~\ref{thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_module_finite_HModule_of_HModule_prime_X4_curve}) — the algebraic counterpart of iter-036, transporting finiteness rather than finrank across the corner-bridge, via Mathlib's $\Module.\mathtt{Finite.equiv}$. Iter-038 → iter-046 build the H⁰ Module.Finite ladder (curve specialisations + carrier predicates + Stein input + global-sections evaluation iso + producer chain end-to-end for $\IsHModuleHomFinite\,k\,C\,(\toModuleKSheaf\,C)$); iter-047 lays foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure; iter-048 appends the Čech-side acyclicity carrier predicate and abstract consumer; iter-049 appends the top-supremum HModule transport (Theorems~\ref{thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_of_isCechAcyclicCover_top} and~\ref{thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_of_isCechAcyclicCover_top_curve}) — the universe $u \to u+1$ bridge that lets iter-053+ ultimately consume iter-048's $\Subsingleton$ on $\HModulePrime$ at the lattice supremum to derive the genus carrier $\Module.\mathtt{Finite}\,k\,(\HModule\,k\,(\toModuleKSheaf\,C)\,1)$ at universe $u+1$; iter-050 packages the Čech-vs-derived comparison iso as a $\Prop$-valued typeclass $\Scheme.\HasCechToHModuleIso$ with $\Classical.\mathtt{choice}$-based extractor (Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_cechToHModuleIso}) and instance-driven \emph{top-supremum} consumers (Theorems~\ref{thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top} and~\ref{thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top_curve}); iter-051 appends the symmetric instance-driven \emph{intermediate-supremum} consumers (Theorems~\ref{thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_prime_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso} and~\ref{thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_prime_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_curve}) — landing $\Subsingleton\,(\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,(\bigsqcup\mathcal U))$ at universe $u$; iter-052 appends the generalised rewrite-bridge $\bigsqcup\mathcal U = U$ consumers (Theorems~\ref{thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_prime_of_hasCechToHModuleIso} and~\ref{thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_prime_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_curve}) — landing $\Subsingleton\,(\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,U)$ on an arbitrary open $U$ under the equality hypothesis; iter-053 appends the existence-bundled affine cover carrier $\HasAffineCechAcyclicCover$ (Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HasAffineCechAcyclicCover}) and the producer $\mathtt{instance}\,\mathtt{instIsAffineHModuleVanishing\_of\_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_instIsAffineHModuleVanishing_of_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover}) — converting iter-040's $\IsAffineHModuleVanishing$ from "user-suppliable hypothesis" to "instance-synthesizable from a single carrier hypothesis", so iter-054+ needs to instantiate ONE class (the carrier) rather than three intertwined obligations; iter-054 appends foundational basic-open cover infrastructure $\mathtt{basicOpenCover}$ (Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_basicOpenCover}), $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_supr\_of\_span\_eq\_top}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top}), $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_isAffineOpen}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen}) — wrapping Mathlib's basic-open cover lemmas into project conventions for iter-055+ to call directly; iter-055 appends the existence-form producer $\mathtt{hasAffineCechAcyclicCover\_of\_basicOpen}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen}) and curve specialisation $\mathtt{hasAffineCechAcyclicCover\_of\_basicOpen\_curve}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen_curve}) — converting per-affine basic-open evidence into iter-053's existence-bundled carrier shape; iter-056 appends pairwise basic-open intersection helpers $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_inter\_eq\_basicOpen\_mul}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul}) and $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_inter\_isAffineOpen}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen}) — packaging the binary-intersection identification + affine-membership transport for iter-057+ Čech-evaluation; iter-057 appends $n$-ary basic-open intersection helpers $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_finset\_inf'\_eq\_basicOpen\_prod}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_finset_inf_eq_basicOpen_prod}) and $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_finset\_inf'\_isAffineOpen}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_finset_inf_isAffineOpen}) — generalising iter-056's binary form to $\mathtt{Finset.inf'}$ indexing matching the Čech complex evaluation at arbitrary degree; iter-058 appends the inclusion helper $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_finset\_inf'\_le}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_finset_inf_le}) supplying $V \le U$ for the $\mathtt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_of\_eq\_basicOpen}$ morphism arg; iter-059 (this iteration) appends the explicit-$@$-algebra localization helper $\mathtt{basicOpenCover\_finset\_inf'\_isLocalization}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_basicOpenCover_finset_inf_isLocalization}) which packages iter-057 + iter-058 by invoking Mathlib's $\mathtt{isLocalization\_of\_eq\_basicOpen}$ — last thin foundational scaffolding step on the affine basic-open identification side. The remaining substantive work consists of: (i) Theorem~\ref{thm:basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf} (Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on an affine basic-open cover, via the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy route); (ii) the parallel Čech-vs-derived comparison statement (a project-local construction since Mathlib has no acyclic-cover-to-derived-functor comparison); (iii) bundling these per-affine into the existence hypothesis of $\mathtt{hasAffineCechAcyclicCover\_of\_basicOpen\_curve}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen_curve}), which then fires the producer for $\IsAffineHModuleVanishing$, chaining through the H⁰ Module-finite ladder + corner-transport to derive the genus carrier $\Module.\mathtt{Finite}\,k\,(\HModule\,k\,(\toModuleKSheaf\,C)\,1)$ that closes Phase A step 6.
